name: enhance_image_tool
description: Pre-processes document images to improve quality before analysis. Use BEFORE calling analyzers when the image shows: faded text, yellowing, noise, low contrast, skew, watermarks, tinted/colored backgrounds (blue security paper, etc.), repeating background text patterns, institutional seals overlapping content, or other degradation. CRITICAL for watermarked documents: official transcripts, certificates, and legal documents on security paper ALWAYS need enhancement — without it, extractors will misidentify content-rich pages as blank or as security/authentication pages. Supports desaturation (remove color tints) and thresholding (binary B&W conversion to strip watermarks/background patterns). For watermarked/security paper documents, use document_type 'watermarked' with 'aggressive' enhancement_level. Returns S3 URI of enhanced image to pass to subsequent analyzers.

image_path.description: S3 URL (s3://bucket/key) or absolute file path to the image to enhance.

image_data.description: Base64-encoded image data. Use this OR image_path, not both.

document_type.description: Type of document for optimized enhancement. 'watermarked' forces desaturate-then-threshold pipeline for security paper, watermarks, colored backgrounds. Default: auto (auto-detect).

session_id.description: Runtime session ID for organizing outputs in S3.

result.description: Enhancement result with S3 URI of enhanced image and processing metadata

s3_output_uri.description: S3 URI of the enhanced image - pass this to subsequent analyzers

skew_angle.description: Detected and corrected skew angle in degrees
